questionnaire.title: Alzheimer's Risk Assessment
questionnaire.subtitle: Complete this comprehensive assessment to understand your risk factors

questions.education: What is your highest education level?
questions.alcohol: How many alcoholic drinks do you have per week?
questions.physicalActivity: How many hours of physical activity do you do per week?
questions.dietQuality: On a scale of 1-10, how would you rate your diet quality?
questions.sleepQuality: On a scale of 1-10, how would you rate your sleep quality?
questions.familyHistory: Do you have a family history of Alzheimer's?
questions.cardiovascular: Have you been diagnosed with cardiovascular disease?
questions.diabetes: Have you been diagnosed with diabetes?
questions.depression: Have you been diagnosed with depression?
questions.headInjury: Have you ever had a significant head injury?
questions.hypertension: Have you been diagnosed with hypertension?
questions.memoryComplaints: Do you experience frequent memory complaints?
questions.confusion: Do you experience episodes of confusion?
questions.disorientation: Do you experience episodes of disorientation?
questions.personalityChanges: Have you noticed changes in your personality?
questions.taskDifficulty: Do you have difficulty completing familiar tasks?
questions.forgetfulness: How would you describe your forgetfulness?

notes.mmse: Take the MMSE test at mmse.neurol.ru and enter your score here.

descriptions.low: Your current risk factors suggest a low probability of developing Alzheimer's disease. Continue maintaining healthy lifestyle habits.
descriptions.medium: Your assessment indicates a moderate risk level. Consider consulting healthcare professionals for personalized advice.
descriptions.high: Your results suggest an elevated risk. We strongly recommend consulting with a healthcare professional for comprehensive evaluation.

games.title: Brain Training Games
games.subtitle: Keep your mind sharp with these cognitive exercises
